examples.title: Basic PGP Key Generation and Management
examples.description: Common GPG command-line operations for key management
examples.code:
# Generate new key pair
gpg --full-generate-key

# List keys
gpg --list-keys
gpg --list-secret-keys

# Export public key
gpg --armor --export user@example.com > public_key.asc

# Import someone else's public key
gpg --import friend_key.asc

# Sign a key (establish trust)
gpg --sign-key friend@example.com

# Verify a key fingerprint
gpg --fingerprint friend@example.com

# Generate revocation certificate
gpg --gen-revoke user@example.com

# Upload key to keyserver
gpg --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --send-keys KEYID

# Search for key on keyserver
gpg --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --search-keys email@example.com
examples.explanation: These are standard GPG commands for everyday PGP operations. The --armor flag produces ASCII-armored output for easy sharing. Key generation uses RSA 4096-bit by default. Fingerprint verification is critical for security.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for key generation where n is key size
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for key storage where n is number of keys

examples.title: PGP Encryption Algorithm
examples.description: Pseudocode showing the complete encryption process with both confidentiality and authentication
examples.code:
FUNCTION PGP_Encrypt(message, sender_private_key, recipient_public_key):
 # Step 1: Generate Digital Signature
 hash = SHA256(message)
 signature = Encrypt_Asymmetric(hash, sender_private_key)
 
 # Step 2: Create signed message
 signed_message = message || signature
 
 # Step 3: Generate random session key
 session_key = Generate_Random_Bytes(256)
 
 # Step 4: Compress (optional but recommended)
 compressed = ZIP(signed_message)
 
 # Step 5: Symmetric encryption of message
 encrypted_message = Encrypt_Symmetric(compressed, session_key)
 
 # Step 6: Asymmetric encryption of session key
 encrypted_session_key = Encrypt_Asymmetric(session_key, recipient_public_key)
 
 # Step 7: Combine into PGP packet
 pgp_message = encrypted_session_key || encrypted_message
 
 RETURN pgp_message

FUNCTION Generate_Random_Bytes(bits):
 # Use cryptographically secure random number generator
 RETURN CSPRNG(bits)

FUNCTION Encrypt_Symmetric(plaintext, key):
 # Use AES-256 in CFB mode
 RETURN AES_Encrypt(plaintext, key)

FUNCTION Encrypt_Asymmetric(plaintext, public_key):
 # Use RSA-4096 or ElGamal
 RETURN RSA_Encrypt(plaintext, public_key)
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the hybrid approach: symmetric encryption for the message (fast) and asymmetric for the session key (secure distribution). The signature is computed before encryption so the recipient can verify the sender's identity.
examples.timeComplexity: O(m log n) where m is message size, n is key size
examples.spaceComplexity: O(m) for message plus O(n) for encrypted session key

examples.title: PGP Decryption and Verification Algorithm
examples.description: Pseudocode showing decryption and signature verification process
examples.code:
FUNCTION PGP_Decrypt(pgp_message, recipient_private_key, sender_public_key):
 # Step 1: Extract components
 encrypted_session_key = pgp_message[0:512] # First 512 bytes
 encrypted_message = pgp_message[512:] # Rest of message
 
 # Step 2: Decrypt session key
 session_key = Decrypt_Asymmetric(encrypted_session_key, recipient_private_key)
 
 # Step 3: Decrypt message
 decrypted = Decrypt_Symmetric(encrypted_message, session_key)
 
 # Step 4: Decompress if needed
 message = UnZIP(decrypted)
 
 # Step 5: Extract message and signature
 message_content = message[0:-256] # Original message
 signature = message[-256:] # Digital signature
 
 # Step 6: Verify signature
 hash_from_signature = Decrypt_Asymmetric(signature, sender_public_key)
 hash_from_message = SHA256(message_content)
 
 IF hash_from_signature == hash_from_message THEN
 RETURN (message_content, VALID_SIGNATURE)
 ELSE
 RETURN (message_content, INVALID_SIGNATURE)
 END IF

FUNCTION Decrypt_Asymmetric(ciphertext, private_key):
 # RSA decryption
 RETURN RSA_Decrypt(ciphertext, private_key)

FUNCTION Decrypt_Symmetric(ciphertext, key):
 # AES decryption
 RETURN AES_Decrypt(ciphertext, key)
examples.explanation: The decryption process reverses encryption: first recover the session key using the recipient's private key, then decrypt the message. Signature verification ensures the message came from the claimed sender and wasn't tampered with. The comparison of hashes validates integrity.
examples.timeComplexity: O(m log n) where m is message size, n is key size
